'''Jordan Williams''' (born 4 June 1997) is an English [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|pr}} or {{rlp|sr}} for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].

He has previously played for the [[London Skolars]] in the [[RFL League 1]].

== Background ==
Williams was born in [[Stevenage]], [[Hertfordshire]] and grew up in the North Hertfordshire market town of [[Hitchin]].

== Career ==
=== Early career ===
Williams played [[rugby union]] as a youth for an amateur rugby union club in Hitchin before transitioning over to [[rugby league]] with the North Herts Crusaders. Following this he went on to play for the North London representative team in the London Origin rugby league series. From there he was scouted by the [[London Broncos]]<ref>{{Cite web |date=2014-06-02 |title=Crusader gets a trial with London Broncos |url=https://www.thecomet.net/sport/crusader-gets-a-trial-with-london-broncos-5293840 |access-date=2022-07-13 |website=The Comet |language=en-UK}}</ref> and progressed through the London Broncos Academy system from 2015 - 2017.

=== London Skolars ===
Williams joined the London Skolars in 2017<ref>{{Cite web |title=London Skolars » Local talent graduate to become Skolars |url=https://www.skolarsrl.com/2016/12/06/local-talent-graduate-to-become-skolars/ |access-date=2022-07-13 |website=www.skolarsrl.com}}</ref> ahead of the [[2017 League 1]] season. He spent three seasons at the [[New River Stadium]], featuring prominently, playing 38 times and scoring 12 tries.

=== London Broncos ===
After a successful 2019 season in North London, achieving top try scorer for the [[London Skolars|Skolars]], Williams signed for the [[London Broncos]] first team at the end of 2019<ref>{{Cite web |last=Conlon |first=Jack |date=2019-11-07 |title=London Broncos snap up former academy player |url=https://www.seriousaboutrl.com/london-broncos-snap-up-former-academy-player-24136/ |access-date=2022-07-13 |website=Rugby League News}}</ref><ref>{{Cite news |title=Williams re-signs for London Broncos |language=en-GB |work=BBC Sport |url=https://www.bbc.com/sport/rugby-league/50332993 |access-date=2022-07-13}}</ref> and made his debut at home on 9 February 2020 against [[Whitehaven R.L.F.C.|Whitehaven]].<ref>{{Cite web |title=London Skolars » Jordan Williams leaves Skolars to sign full-time contract with Championship club |url=https://www.skolarsrl.com/2019/11/07/jordan-williams-leaves-skolars-to-sign-full-time-contract-with-championship-club/ |access-date= |website=}}</ref>
[[File:Jordan Williams London Broncos.jpg|thumb|right|Williams playing for the Broncos in 2022]]
On 15 October 2023, Williams played in the London Broncos upset [[Million Pound Game]] victory over [[Toulouse Olympique]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67105947|title=Championship Grand Final: Toulouse 14-18 London Broncos - Capital club back in Super League|work=BBC Sport }}</ref>

===Widnes Vikings (loan)===
On 12 July 2024 it was reported that he had signed for [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] in the [[RFL Championship]] on loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=12 Jul 2024|author=Drew Darbyshire|title=Super League loan watch: London Broncos forward heads to Widnes Vikings|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/super-league-players-who-have-agreed-loan-dual-registration-deals}}</ref>

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 19 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers|Featherstone]] in the RFL Championship on a one-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=19 Nov 2024|author=Ben Olawumi|title=Championship club swoop to sign forward following London Broncos exit|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/championship-club-swoop-to-sign-forward-following-london-broncos-exit}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 14 August 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] on a 2-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=14 Aug 2025|accessdate=27 Sep 2025|title=Wakefield sign Featherstone front rower Williams|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/articles/c4gm39j72j8o}}</ref>
